Missing Bolt Detection

Catching Assembly and Maintenance Oversights

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

The Problem

Cooling pumps are assembled with multiple bolts to secure the pump housing, impeller, and motor. During assembly or maintenance, a bolt can be accidentally omitted, creating an imbalance and structural weakness.

This defect is often invisible during visual inspection and won't cause immediate failure, but will lead to vibration, wear, and eventual catastrophic failure.

The Detection

When a cooling pump is missing a bolt, its structural imbalance creates distinctive electrical signatures in the motor:

  • Consistent vibration at the rotational frequency and harmonics
  • Irregular waveform patterns that don't match a healthy pump
  • Increased harmonic content indicating mechanical imbalance
  • Changes in the current envelope that reveal structural issues

Samotics' algorithms detected this fault immediately upon startup, before the pump could operate.

The Benefit

This early detection prevented the pump from operating in a damaged state, which would have caused rapid wear, bearing damage, and eventual failure in production. Quality and safety were both improved.
